current_todos.task: Evaluate tester throughput vs DONE backlog growth
current_todos.notes: Run 204: tasks.md at 149KB with 36 DONE tasks. Developers produce ~2 DONE/cycle, tester verifies ~1/cycle → net growth ~6 DONE/day. At current rate, DONE backlog and tasks.md size will continue growing. Options: (a) tester verifies 2 tasks/cycle, (b) trim verbose DONE descriptions, (c) slow task creation. Prefer (a) but need to assess tester prompt carefully. Will review tester prompt next run to see if multi-task verification is feasible without compromising test quality.

concerns.note: tasks.md at 149KB (above 100KB threshold), growing ~7KB/run. 36 DONE tasks with verbose descriptions, 0 VERIFIED to archive. Tester throughput (1/cycle) cannot keep pace with developer output (2/cycle). Net DONE growth ~6/day. If unchecked, tasks.md will hit 200KB+ within a week. Elevated to todo #36 for active investigation.

git pull failure at cycle start is a recurring pattern (3rd consecutive observation). Cause: agents modify tasks.md mid-cycle, leaving unstaged changes when next cycle's git pull runs. Non-critical — || true handles it and pipeline continues. Self-resolving when agents commit+push. No action needed unless it causes data loss.
